data.narrative:
Defense Unicorns, a Series B defense and security technology company founded by Rob Slaughter, Jeff McCoy, and Andrew Greene, is currently valued at $1.0B post-money following $171M in total capital raised. Valuation analysis draws from a universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private—spanning the Defense & Security sector at equivalent stages.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $1.0B last post-money valuation, implying $1.9B. Peer-median analysis across 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with the peer set ranging from $300M to $3.0B. The comparable set includes Dream Security ($3.0B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Code Metal ($1.2B), Castelion ($1.0B), and Heven AeroTech ($1.0B).

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $956M to $2.2B, with a median of $1.5B. The current $1.0B valuation sits near the lower bound. Key uncertainties include the heterogeneity within the defense tech peer set—evidenced by the $300M-to-$3.0B spread—and the limited visibility into stage-specific capital deployment timelines and burn profiles across comparables. The wide peer dispersion suggests material differences in technology maturity, contract pipeline, or customer concentration that warrant further due diligence.
